text.title: CoT Techniques and Selection
text.summary: CoT variants, selection decision matrix, cost/quality trade-offs, and 2025 research insights.

rule.statement: Default to zero-shot CoT for modern models (Claude Sonnet 4+, GPT-4+). Add "Think step by step" or use Claude's `<thinking>` tags. Use few-shot CoT only when a specific output format must be enforced — not for reasoning quality improvement.
rule.rationale: 2025 research (arXiv:2506.14641) shows zero-shot CoT matches or exceeds few-shot CoT for strong models. Few-shot examples primarily enforce output format. DeepSeek-R1 shows performance degradation with few-shot CoT.

rule.statement: Use Self-Consistency (5 samples, temperature=0.7, majority vote) only for mission-critical single decisions — not for conversational or high-volume pipelines. Budget 5x token cost explicitly before enabling.
rule.rationale: Self-consistency improves accuracy on ambiguous problems by sampling diverse reasoning paths. At 5 samples/query it costs 5x tokens — only justified when the cost of a wrong answer exceeds the cost of the extra API calls.

rule.statement: Use Tree-of-Thoughts (ToT) only for narrow planning or exploration problems (e.g., code planning, game-like search). Branching factor 3-5, max depth 3-5, best-first search. Never use as a general-purpose technique.
rule.rationale: ToT's branching^depth call count grows exponentially. At branching=3, depth=3 it requires up to 39 LLM calls per query. The 70% improvement on Game of 24 does not generalize to most production tasks.

rule.statement: Use Extended Thinking (Claude Opus + `thinking` parameter) for math, formal logic, code correctness proofs, and multi-constraint optimization. Start at 1,024 budget tokens; increase by 1,024 until quality plateaus.
rule.rationale: Extended Thinking provides verifiable reasoning traces, not just CoT scaffolding. Diminishing returns appear quickly — most tasks plateau before 8,192 thinking tokens.

list.item: Simple task + format flexible → Zero-Shot CoT ("Think step by step")
Simple task + strict format → Few-Shot CoT (2-3 examples)
Complex + single critical answer → Self-Consistency (5 samples)
Zero-Shot CoT: low cost, low latency, good quality
Few-Shot CoT: medium cost (examples in prompt), medium latency, good+ quality
Tree-of-Thoughts: very high cost, very high latency, best quality for planning
Extended Thinking: +$0.30/call (Opus at 4K thinking tokens), verifiable traces
